title: [SimplifyCFG] Drop !invariant.group when merging conditional stores
author: jlebar
description:
mergeConditionalStoreToAddress changes e.g.

    if (cond) store ptr, x;
    else store ptr, y;

into

    str ptr, select(cond, x, y);

In doing so, it would set !invariant.group on the store if *either* of
the stores had !invariant.group.  That was an assertion that the value
being stored was the same for every other load/store to the same pointer
that is also marked with invariant.group.  That's incorrect, because the
two stores could be (in fact, likely *are*) storing different values.

This bug was found by a large run of Opus 4.7 looking for bugs in LLVM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

tests.test_body:
define void @merge_keeps_invariant_group_when_both(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, ptr %p, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %t1, label %m

t1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  store i32 %a, ptr %p, align 4, !invariant.group !0
  br label %m

m:                                                ; preds = %t1, %entry
  br i1 %c2, label %t2, label %e

t2:                                               ; preds = %m
  store i32 %b, ptr %p, align 4, !invariant.group !0
  br label %e

e:                                                ; preds = %t2, %m
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

define void @merge_drops_invariant_group_when_asymmetric(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, ptr %p, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %t1, label %m

t1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  store i32 %a, ptr %p, align 4, !invariant.group !0
  br label %m

m:                                                ; preds = %t1, %entry
  br i1 %c2, label %t2, label %e

t2:                                               ; preds = %m
  store i32 %b, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %e

e:                                                ; preds = %t2, %m
  ret void
}

!0 = !{}

comments.author: llvmorg-github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM PR SUMMARY COMMENT-->

@llvm/pr-subscribers-llvm-transforms

Author: Justin Lebar (jlebar)

<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

mergeConditionalStoreToAddress changes e.g.

    if (cond) store ptr, x;
    else store ptr, y;

into

    str ptr, select(cond, x, y);

In doing so, it would set !invariant.group on the store if *either* of
the stores had !invariant.group.  That was an assertion that the value
being stored was the same for every other load/store to the same pointer
that is also marked with invariant.group.  That's incorrect, because the
two stores could be (in fact, likely *are*) storing different values.

This bug was found by a large run of Opus 4.7 looking for bugs in LLVM.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@<!-- -->anthropic.com>


---
Full diff: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/200551.diff


2 Files Affected:

- (modified) llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp (+18) 
- (modified) llvm/test/Transforms/SimplifyCFG/merge-cond-stores.ll (+60) 


``````````diff
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp
index a8fd9f4a10064..fa5af8b402c26 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Utils/SimplifyCFG.cpp
@@ -4456,9 +4456,27 @@ static bool mergeConditionalStoreToAddress(
   }
 
   QB.SetInsertPoint(T);
+
+  // The merged store's metadata is set via combineMetadataForCSE, below.  This
+  // does not give quite the correct metadata, though.  In particular, that
+  // function assumes the two stores being combined are semantically
+  // equivalent, whereas the whole point of this transformation is that the
+  // stores write different values.
+  //
+  // In particular, combineMetadataForCSE sets !invariant.group if it's present
+  // on either store, but we should set it only if it's present on *both*.
+  //
+  // TODO: Maybe it would be better not to use combineMetadataForCSE at all?
+  bool KeepInvariantGroup =
+      PStore->getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_invariant_group) &&
+      QStore->getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_invariant_group);
+
   StoreInst *SI = cast<StoreInst>(QB.CreateStore(QPHI, Address));
   combineMetadataForCSE(QStore, PStore, true);
   SI->copyMetadata(*QStore);
+  if (!KeepInvariantGroup)
+    SI->setMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_invariant_group, nullptr);
+
   // Update any dbg.assign intrinsics to track the merged value (QPHI) instead
   // of the original constant values, likely making these identical.
   for (auto *DbgAssign : at::getDVRAssignmentMarkers(SI)) {
diff --git a/llvm/test/Transforms/SimplifyCFG/merge-cond-stores.ll b/llvm/test/Transforms/SimplifyCFG/merge-cond-stores.ll
index b1cce4484bbab..549eeeba35e14 100644
--- a/llvm/test/Transforms/SimplifyCFG/merge-cond-stores.ll
+++ b/llvm/test/Transforms/SimplifyCFG/merge-cond-stores.ll
@@ -409,6 +409,66 @@ end:
 
 !0 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 7, i32 13}
 !1 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 3, i32 11}
+
+; When both merged stores carry the same !invariant.group, every path already
+; asserted that its stored value was the group's invariant value, so the merged
+; store may keep !invariant.group.
+define void @merge_keeps_invariant_group_when_both(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, ptr %p, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @merge_keeps_invariant_group_when_both(
+; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP0:%.*]] = or i1 [[C1:%.*]], [[C2:%.*]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[TMP0]], label [[TMP1:%.*]], label [[TMP2:%.*]]
+; CHECK:       1:
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SPEC_SELECT:%.*]] = select i1 [[C2]], i32 [[B:%.*]], i32 [[A:%.*]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    store i32 [[SPEC_SELECT]], ptr [[P:%.*]], align 4, !invariant.group [[META1:![0-9]+]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[TMP2]]
+; CHECK:       2:
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
+;
+entry:
+  br i1 %c1, label %t1, label %m
+t1:
+  store i32 %a, ptr %p, !invariant.group !2
+  br label %m
+m:
+  br i1 %c2, label %t2, label %e
+t2:
+  store i32 %b, ptr %p, !invariant.group !2
+  br label %e
+e:
+  ret void
+}
+
+; When only one of the merged stores carries !invariant.group, keeping it would
+; assert the invariant on the other store's path, which never promised it. The
+; merged store must drop !invariant.group.
+define void @merge_drops_invariant_group_when_asymmetric(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, ptr %p, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: @merge_drops_invariant_group_when_asymmetric(
+; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP0:%.*]] = or i1 [[C1:%.*]], [[C2:%.*]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 [[TMP0]], label [[TMP1:%.*]], label [[TMP2:%.*]]
+; CHECK:       1:
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[SPEC_SELECT:%.*]] = select i1 [[C2]], i32 [[B:%.*]], i32 [[A:%.*]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    store i32 [[SPEC_SELECT]], ptr [[P:%.*]], align 4
+; CHECK-NEXT:    br label [[TMP2]]
+; CHECK:       2:
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
+;
+entry:
+  br i1 %c1, label %t1, label %m
+t1:
+  store i32 %a, ptr %p, !invariant.group !2
+  br label %m
+m:
+  br i1 %c2, label %t2, label %e
+t2:
+  store i32 %b, ptr %p
+  br label %e
+e:
+  ret void
+}
+!2 = !{}
 ;.
 ; CHECK: [[PROF0]] = !{!"branch_weights", i32 137, i32 143}
+; CHECK: [[META1]] = !{}
 ;.

``````````

</details>
